description: Fix the flaky strict-csp.e2e.ts Chromium launch timeout by increasing the launch timeout and reducing peak resource contention during parallel execution.

userStories.title: Increase Chromium launch timeout in E2E fixtures from 30s to 60s
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the Chromium launch timeout to be generous enough to survive resource contention during parallel E2E test execution so that tests don't fail due to slow Chromium startup.
acceptanceCriteria: The timeout parameter in chromium.launchPersistentContext is increased from 30_000 to 60_000 in e2e/fixtures.ts
userStories.notes:
Single line change in `e2e/fixtures.ts` at the `launchExtensionContext` function (around line 916).

Change `timeout: 30_000` to `timeout: 60_000`.

Rationale: With `fullyParallel: true` and 4 workers, up to 4 Chromium instances launch simultaneously. Each Chromium process takes significant CPU and memory at startup. On loaded machines (or when multiple workers happen to launch Chromium at the same moment), 30s can be insufficient. The per-test timeout is already 120s, so a 60s launch timeout still leaves 60s for the actual test logic.

The strict-csp tests are especially prone because each test spawns 3 subprocesses (MCP server + strict-CSP server + Chromium) versus 2 for most other tests, creating more resource pressure.

This is a targeted fix at the specific timeout that failed. The alternative of serializing strict-csp tests would slow down the suite unnecessarily — the flake is rare and caused by resource contention, not a test isolation bug.
